divergences: Desktop wallpaper is a CSS gradient approximation (radial/linear gradients sampled from the reference photo) of a continuous photographic nebula texture; fine diagonal streak detail and grain are not reproduced pixel-exact, especially in the strip directly above the window.

Window vibrancy is emulated with backdrop-filter(saturate+blur) over a dedicated flat ambient-wash layer sized to the window rect (tuned to match measured mid-list-row colors ~rgb(50,27,76)); this stands in for what would be a true Gaussian blur of the actual wallpaper bitmap behind the window, so very local hue drift across the window interior is smoothed out rather than exactly reproduced.

Codex row icon is a hand-drawn inline-SVG approximation (white tile, blue-to-purple gradient cloud/blob glyph with a '>_' mark) of the real OpenAI Codex app icon; shape is a close visual match, not a vector-exact trace.

Font rendering antialiasing/hinting differs slightly between the JPG capture's source renderer and the headless Chromium used for the HTML recreation, contributing sub-pixel edge noise across all text.
Window border/shadow softness (box-shadow blur radius) is a measured approximation, not a pixel-exact match of the reference's compositor shadow falloff.
